themes.exampleQuote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
I'm an ice cream snob and this is some of the best ice cream I've ever had.
The owner is extremely friendly and nice and helped make the experience that much better.
The comment along the lines of 'I'm not going to waste any more ice cream on you all,' delivered in a rude tone, made us feel uncomfortable.
After our first bite we were already excited to plan on coming back again.

notable_quote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently praise the ice cream as benchmark-level and the Liège waffle as a genuine rarity in Pittsburgh; the friction point is an unwritten sample policy that has surfaced more than once and cost at least two customers their enthusiasm.
quarter_narrative: Thirty-two Pittsburgh creators posted TikToks at Treat this quarter, pulling in more than 1.1 million plays, and the business posted zero videos of its own. That gap is the whole story of Spring 2026 for this Shadyside shop: the product is clearly compelling enough to drive repeat visits from creators without any coordination, but the business has no account to funnel that attention into. Meanwhile, Treat's own Instagram has been quiet for roughly 200 days, leaving a 7.65 percent engagement rate sitting on a dormant feed. In a family where Page's holds the lead on the strength of social momentum, Treat's rank at seventh in Pittsburgh Sweets reflects a business that the city is clearly interested in but that has not yet figured out how to stay in the room after the waffle is gone. Reactivating the Instagram feed and staking a claim on TikTok would convert existing creator energy into a durable signal rather than a series of one-time spikes.
tldr_read: Over a million TikTok plays from 32 creators, a dormant Instagram, and a service friction thread in the reviews. Ones to Watch, #3 in Pittsburgh Ice Cream.
tldr_meaning: The path upward is not about product, which the data already validates; it is about whether the Instagram signal restarts and whether the business begins capturing any portion of the TikTok attention it is already generating.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Third In Ice Cream, Built On Creator Attention. The Business Has Yet To Meet It.

playbook.headline: Claim a TikTok presence now
playbook.action: Thirty-two creators generated 1.1 million plays this quarter with no account to land on; creating a profile captures that traffic before it dissolves.

playbook.headline: Restart the Instagram feed this week
playbook.action: The account has a 7.65 percent engagement rate but has posted nothing in 200 days; one post per week would reactivate a signal that is already warmed up.

playbook.headline: Post a clear samples policy in-store
playbook.action: Two separate review threads cite the sample limit as the reason they did not complete an order; a visible posted policy removes the ambiguity before it reaches Google.
